DEEPCAST · ITALIC SERIF CANDIDATES · prologue lines at shipped size (31px cap), warm #dddddd on ink #080c18
Sheet is self-contained (fonts embedded). Slant angles measured from each font file’s post table. 2026-07-21.
A · Garamond Premier Pro Italic — AS SHIPPED
true glyph slant −18° PLUS the browser’s synthetic oblique stacked on top (the CSS asks for italic on a face declared normal) — effective slant ≈ 30°

Jon, Ien watched this and thought of you.

No algorithm decided you should see it. A real person did.

This is the bug hiding under the complaint: the steepest panel on this sheet is not the font alone.
B · Garamond Premier Pro Italic — SAME FONT, synthesis off + 5% larger + slight tracking
the cut’s honest −18°, no synthetic skew, +0.01em letter-spacing

Jon, Ien watched this and thought of you.

No algorithm decided you should see it. A real person did.

Zero-cost option: one CSS line (font-synthesis: none) removes the double slant everywhere.
C · Crimson Pro Italic — Google Fonts (OFL)
slant −12° · old-style Garalde DNA, closest voice to the current brand

Jon, Ien watched this and thought of you.

No algorithm decided you should see it. A real person did.

A third gentler than the shipped look while staying unmistakably classical.
D · Lora Italic — Google Fonts (OFL)
slant −3° · calligraphic but nearly upright, sturdy strokes

Jon, Ien watched this and thought of you.

No algorithm decided you should see it. A real person did.

The most phone-legible of the five; trades some old-world delicacy for clarity.
E · Literata Italic — Google Fonts (OFL)
slant −2° · bookish upright-cursive, variable optical size tuned for small screens

Jon, Ien watched this and thought of you.

No algorithm decided you should see it. A real person did.

Designed for long reading on phones (Google Play Books); gentlest slant of all.
